betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Airborne Scouts: Drones§f

§aConstruct light, fast, and unconstrained flying micro-computers to relay data and survey terrain!§f
Drones are highly agile, flying entities. While they share some features with robots, they are optimized for air mobility and remote operations.

§6Drone Capabilities & Flight§f:
§b• Unconstrained Flight§f: Unlike robots, drones have §ano flight height limits§f. They can fly freely to any altitude.
§b• Energy Buffer§f: Drones have a smaller internal capacity of §e5,000 energy§f.
§b• Active Cost§f: A running drone constantly consumes §e0.4 energy per tick§f.
§b• Constraints§f: Drones are too small to support screens or keyboards. You cannot interact with them directly via terminal. They must be controlled remotely.

§6Remote Control & Communication§f:
Because you cannot type commands on a drone, you must write scripts on a separate computer to send wireless instructions:
1. Equip the drone with a §bWireless Network Card§f.
2. Send network packets (using the §9modem API§f) from a base station computer.
3. The drone listens for these signals and runs local code based on the instructions received.

§6Assembly Specifications§f:
Assemble drones in the §bRobot Assembler§f using a §bDrone Case§f. 
§eDrone Assembly Cost§f: §e25,000 base energy§f + §e15,000 energy per complexity point§f. Keep component counts optimized to speed up manufacturing!
